confirmed.issue: Per-slice route-back budget never persists: runSlice declares `let routeBacks = 0` (line 259) but every honored route-back re-enters via `return await runSlice(ph, slice)` (lines 277, 291, 302), resetting the counter to 0 each frame, so `routeBacks < cfg.maxRouteBacks` is permanently true and a single mis-planned slice recurses until it drains the whole-initiative maxAgentCalls (400) budget instead of bounding at 4.
confirmed.fix: Thread the counter across re-entries. Change line 253 to `async function runSlice(ph, slice, routeBacks = 0) {`, delete `let routeBacks = 0` at line 259, and in each recursive re-entry pass the incremented value: replace `return await runSlice(ph, slice)` at lines 277, 291, 302 with `return await runSlice(ph, slice, routeBacks)` (the existing `routeBacks++` before each call now accumulates). The external caller at line 329 keeps `await runSlice(ph, slice)` (default 0). This makes the maxRouteBacks ceiling real and makes the escalate/blocked fallbacks at 278/292-293/303 reachable so an exhausted slice degrades gracefully.

confirmed.issue: S1 upstream route-back is never re-entered: the ROUTEBACK schema (line 113) and modules (s6-verify-phase.md names S1 the MOST COMMON S6 route-back; s4-build.md, s3-verify-plan.md) all permit/produce targetStep:'S1', and DESIGN §6/§10 promise the engine 'actually re-enters S0/S1/S2', but runInitiative's escalate handler (line 380) re-enters ONLY targetStep==='S0'; every S1 escalate falls to line 385 and is silently abandoned as an 'unhandled upstream route-back' blocker. There is no `=== 'S1'` re-entry site anywhere in the engine.
confirmed.fix: Add an S1 branch to the escalate handler (lines 378-386) that re-runs ONLY the affected phase (not a full S0 restart), bounded by its own counter. `ph` and `singlePhase` are already in scope, so no phaseId lookup is needed. Introduce `let phaseReentries = 0` near line 361 and `maxPhaseReentries: a.maxPhaseReentries ?? 2` in cfg (line 36). In the handler, keep the existing S0 branch (`return await runInitiative()`), then add: when `r.escalate.targetStep === 'S1' && phaseReentries < cfg.maxPhaseReentries`, increment phaseReentries, log, `const rr = await runPhase(ph, singlePhase)` (runPhase re-runs S1 -> slices -> S6, the cascade DESIGN §6 wants), promote a still-S0 escalation from rr to the S0 path if budget remains, then `continue`. Otherwise fall through to the existing blocker note. Acceptable alternative: narrow DESIGN §6/§10 and s6-verify-phase.md to state only S0 is re-entered at the boundary and S1 degrades to a blocker by design, so docs and engine agree.

confirmed.issue: A built:true S4 tier-promotion route-back is silently dropped: per risk-tiering.md §5 and s4-build.md §2/gate routing, a slice promoted to CRITICAL at S4 returns built:true PLUS routeBack:{targetStep:'S2'} so the engine re-routes it into the FULL lane to gain S3 plan-verify, but the S2/S3 re-entry in runSlice is gated inside `if (!build.built)` (line 289), so with built:true the routeBack is ignored, the slice proceeds straight to S5, and S3 plan-verify NEVER runs for a slice promoted at S4. SCHEMA_S4 also omits riskTier, so a no-routeBack promotion is unobservable.
confirmed.fix: Two changes in strike-v2.mjs. (1) Add `riskTier: { type: 'string', description: 'TRIVIAL|STANDARD|CRITICAL — present iff raised' }` to SCHEMA_S4 (line 135-137). (2) In runSlice, lift the S2/S3 routeBack re-entry OUT of the `if (!build.built)` gate (line 289) and also trigger it on a CRITICAL promotion of a non-FULL slice, regardless of built: after `const s4route = routeSplitOrObstruction(build, sid, slice); if (s4route) return s4route`, compute `const rbTarget = build.routeBack && build.routeBack.targetStep` and `const promotedToFull = build.riskTier === 'CRITICAL' && lane !== 'FULL'`; if `(rbTarget === 'S2' || rbTarget === 'S3' || promotedToFull) && routeBacks < cfg.maxRouteBacks`, increment routeBacks, persist the promotion (`slice.riskTier='CRITICAL'; slice.lane='FULL'`) so the re-run takes the FULL lane and S3 runs, logRouteBack, and `return await runSlice(ph, slice, routeBacks)`. Keep the existing built:false blocker path below.

confirmed.issue: S7 final-gate route-back is only logged, never acted on: at lines 394-395 runInitiative sets report.ready and, on final.status==='routeback', calls logRouteBack('S7', final.routeBack) and stops — it never re-enters any target. verifyWithFix legitimately surfaces S7 route-backs (line 205, routeBack is in the authoritative schema), and DESIGN §6/§10 plus s7-verify-main-spec.md promise the engine re-enters the target and cascades. A repairable final-gate FAIL therefore silently terminates as not-ready — the exact last-gate hole v2 claims to close.
confirmed.fix: Route the S7 route-back through the same bounded escalate path used for phase escalations. Replace lines 392-395 so that when `final.status === 'routeback'`: logRouteBack('S7', final.routeBack); if `final.routeBack.targetStep === 'S0' && upstreamRouteBacks < cfg.maxUpstreamRouteBacks`, increment upstreamRouteBacks, log, and `return await runInitiative()` (full re-entry + implicit cascade via artifact overwrite); otherwise `note(report.blockers, { where: 'S7', reason: 'unhandled final-gate route-back to '+final.routeBack.targetStep+' (budget '+upstreamRouteBacks+'/'+cfg.maxUpstreamRouteBacks+')', detail: final.routeBack.reason })`. Keep `report.ready = final.status === 'passed'` in the non-routeback branch. (Full S1/S2/S4 final-gate re-entry depends on the phase/slice re-run mechanism from the S1 fix; the S0 case is the minimal change that stops the silent terminate.)

confirmed.issue: On-disk slices/ reconciliation is unimplemented: DESIGN §6/§9.1/§10, s1/s2 step contracts, obstruction-loop.md §4, and the engine's OWN comment at line 309 ('S1 slices -> reconcile -> run slices') all declare the engine reconciles the on-disk `slices/` dir as source of truth for deterministic resume after a split, but the engine has zero filesystem access (no fs import; the slice list is purely in-memory `s1.slices` at line 321, mutated by splice at 333-334). On split/array-vs-disk divergence, agent-written slice dirs are orphaned, and an upstream S0 re-entry (`return await runInitiative()`, line 383) discards the in-memory slices array entirely, so 'deterministic resume after a split' never happens.
confirmed.fix: Add `import { readdirSync, existsSync } from 'node:fs'` at the top of strike-v2.mjs and a `reconcileSlices(pid, inMemory)` helper that reads subdirectories of `${P.sliceDir(pid,'').replace(/\/$/,'')}` (phases/<pid>/slices/) containing a slice.md, treats disk as source of truth for slice EXISTENCE/order, carries in-memory metadata by id where present, and synthesizes a minimal stub `{ id, name: id, lane: 'FAST' }` for disk-only ids (guarded by existsSync so first pass is a no-op). Call it in runPhase right after `let slices = (s1.slices || []).slice()` (line 321) and again after each split/enabling splice (lines 333-334), preserving the loop cursor. This makes disk-written-but-not-returned slices build and makes resume-after-split deterministic across the runInitiative() re-entry. Acceptable alternative: if in-memory-only resume is the real intent, delete the 'on-disk dir as source of truth' / 'deterministic resume' wording from DESIGN §6/§9.1/§10, s1/s2, obstruction-loop.md, and the line 309 comment, and mandate the splitter agent write each replacement's slice.md before returning.

confirmed.issue: Split replacement slices get no slice.md stub, so the next S2 reads a missing file: SCHEMA_S2.replacementSlices items carry only {id,name,lane} (line 133), S1 only wrote slice.md for the ORIGINAL ids (line 317), and neither the engine nor the s2-plan.md split contract writes a stub for a new replacement id — yet after a split the next S2 is told to 'Read the slice stub ${P.slice(pid,sid)}' (line 263). A split with a new id leaves S2 reading a missing/stale slice.md with no reconciliation to repair it. (Resolved together with, and dependent on, the reconciliation fix above.)
confirmed.fix: Either (a) widen SCHEMA_S2.replacementSlices items (line 133) to carry enough fields to write a usable stub (behavior/acceptanceCriteria/footprint/size) and, when reconcileSlices finds a replacement id with no slice.md on disk, write a minimal stub from the returned Slice fields before the loop re-enters S2; OR (b) mandate in strike-engine-v2/steps/s2-plan.md that the splitter agent itself writes each replacement's slice.md (and S1 writes original stubs) before returning replacementSlices, so 'Read the slice stub' at line 263 is always satisfiable. Pick one and make SCHEMA_S2, the engine reconciliation, and s2-plan.md agree.

confirmed.issue: Engine-side obstruction corroboration / conservative reclassification is missing: obstruction-loop.md §3-4 and DESIGN §1.9/§7 assign the ENGINE the job of re-classifying obstructions upward ('unknown => one-way/Tier 3'; money/migration/auth/security/external-effects/idempotency are one-way by rule; 'the engine routes on tier but corroborates, not on trust'), but routeSplitOrObstruction (lines 239-247) routes solely on the raw agent-declared `r.obstruction.tier`; reversibility/blastRadius/surfaces are read only for the report (lines 93,95) and never gate routing. An agent that under-declares a one-way obstruction as Tier 1/2 routes straight through with zero engine challenge.
confirmed.fix: Add an engine-side conservative-corroboration pass that runs on every obstruction BEFORE routing, using only already-available structured fields. At the top of routeSplitOrObstruction, call a `corroborateObstruction(r)` that: builds the declared surfaces set from `o.surfaces` + `r.surfaces` (lowercased); if `o.reversibility` is 'unknown'/absent/'one-way' OR any surface matches a one-way-by-rule list (money, migration(s), auth, security, external-effect(s), idempotency, pii) and `o.tier !== 3`, logs the forced promotion, notes it in report.obstructions, and sets `o.tier = 3`. Then the existing Tier-3 branch (line 243) diverts the under-declared obstruction away from the silent enabling-split / pass-through paths. Do NOT implement a footprint-vs-changedFiles diff check here — the engine has no structured slice footprint and no diff access; that corroboration requires first adding a `footprint: string[]` field to the S1 slice schema, or belongs to the S5 verifier which already reads the diff.

confirmed.issue: Channel-B zero-progress is measured on a single headline string, not the failing-criteria SET: DESIGN §7 and obstruction-loop.md §1 specify revert-and-reset fires when no acceptance criterion moves FAIL->PASS across attempts (and stake an anti-gaming property on measuring the criteria SET, not test churn), but the engine compares only `v.failedCriterion === lastFailedCriterion` (line 211) and the schema (ENVELOPE line 119, SCHEMA_VERDICT line 138) carries only a single `failedCriterion` string with no per-criterion array. A verifier that surfaces a DIFFERENT single criterion each round makes noProgress always false, so revert-and-reset never fires and the slice silently exhausts maxFixAttempts to a blocker — the exact perturb-the-target evasion the discipline claims immunity to (and a symmetric inverse that wrongly trips reset).
confirmed.fix: Make the verifier return the failing-criteria set and measure progress on it. Extend SCHEMA_VERDICT (line 138) with `failedCriteria: STR` (every still-FAILing named criterion this attempt; STR is defined at line 104). In verifyWithFix, track the prior set and set `noProgress` true when the new failing-criteria set did not shrink (no criterion moved FAIL->PASS) between attempts, instead of the single-string equality at lines 211-212. Optionally document failedCriteria in disciplines/honest-verification.md so verifier agents populate it; the SCHEMA_VERDICT field is what makes it authoritative.

confirmed.issue: SCHEMA_FIX omits the `obstruction` and `blockers` envelope fields that the engine's absorb() actively reads: fix.md §7 declares a fix step returns `obstruction` (set on a Tier-3 escalation, carrying adrPath) and `blockers[]` (genuinely unrepairable, e.g. unsafe interim), and absorb() (lines 90,92,93) populates report.blockers, report.obstructions, and report.adrs from them — but SCHEMA_FIX (lines 139-141) lists only {fixed,artifactPath,changedFiles,assumptions,remaining,routeBack,reason}. Because the schema is authoritative at runtime, a Tier-3 fix escalation cannot conformantly return obstruction, so report.obstructions/report.adrs are never populated from a fix step and the precise per-item blockers[] content is lost (only the generic fx.reason fallback survives), violating DESIGN §10 auditable-report integrity.
confirmed.fix: In SCHEMA_FIX (lines 139-141) add the two envelope fields absorb() reads: `obstruction: OBSTRUCTION` and `blockers: STR` (both OBSTRUCTION at line 105 and STR at line 104 are already in scope above SCHEMA_FIX). This lets a Tier-3 fix escalation return its obstruction (so absorb populates report.obstructions and, via obstruction.adrPath, report.adrs) and lets an unsafe-interim fix return its precise blockers[] content. Report-only change; routing (fixed/routeBack) is unaffected.

overall: Conditionally production-ready after these fixes, with one structural caveat. All ten confirmed findings live in a single file (the engine, strike-v2.mjs) and are localized, well-understood routing/budget/schema defects with concrete minimal patches — not architectural rewrites. The one true correctness/termination bug is P0 #1 (per-slice route-back counter resets on recursion, so the per-slice budget is non-functional and a pathological slice drains the global agent-call ceiling); it must ship first because every other route-back fix (#2 S1 re-entry, #3 S4 promotion re-route, #4 S7 re-entry) re-enters runSlice and relies on the counter actually accumulating. Notably the run still TERMINATES today even with the P0 present (the global maxAgentCalls backstop and graceful-degradation-to-blocker exist throughout), so there is no hang/infinite-loop risk — this is why the engine is salvageable rather than fundamentally broken. The structural concern worth flagging for the author: v2's marquee feature is 'upstream re-entry the engine actually executes,' yet as written the engine genuinely re-enters only S0 — S1/S2/S4/S7 route-backs are dropped to blockers (#2, #4) and the on-disk reconciliation/deterministic-resume contract is entirely unimplemented (#5/#6, the engine has zero filesystem access). These are a recurring pattern of contract-vs-implementation drift (DESIGN/modules promise behaviors the engine omits), so beyond the patches the team should either (a) build out the phase/slice re-run + on-disk reconciliation machinery so the engine matches its contract, or (b) explicitly narrow DESIGN §6/§9.1/§10 and the S6/S7 modules to the S0-only reality. Either is acceptable, but the two must be reconciled before v2 is trustworthy in production; shipping the four P1s and five P2s without closing that doc-vs-code gap leaves the engine quietly weaker than its own design claims. Ordering: P0 #1 -> P1s #2/#3/#4 (build on #1) -> P2s #5-#10 (independent, can land in any order). Files touched: only /Users/cracklehat/Sites/workflow-exploration/.claude/workflows/strike-v2.mjs for code, plus doc/contract edits under strike-engine-v2/DESIGN.md, strike-engine-v2/steps/{s1,s2,s7}-*.md, and strike-engine-v2/disciplines/{obstruction-loop,honest-verification}.md for the alternative doc-narrowing resolutions of #2/#5/#6/#8/#9.
